label: Bamako
sub: Mali's capital and seat of the Goïta junta. Site of the 2020 and 2021 coups; Africa Corps HQ; JNIM fuel blockade target since 2024. Kati garrison 15 km away struck by JNIM Sept 2024.

label: Ouagadougou
sub: Burkina Faso's capital; site of two coups in 2022. Ibrahim Traoré's junta HQ. Africa Corps troops present. ~40% of national territory estimated outside government control.

label: Niamey
sub: Niger's capital; site of July 2023 coup by Gen. Tchiani. Former Operation Barkhane sub-HQ. US forces expelled April–August 2024. Russian military advisers now present.

label: Gao
sub: Former MUJAO stronghold liberated January 2013. Major French Barkhane base (Camp Firgun). Now the Africa Corps/FAMa operational hub for northern Mali. Starting point of the 2023 Kidal offensive column.

label: Kidal
sub: Former Tuareg/MNLA stronghold. Secured by French forces January 30, 2013. Fell to CSP-PSD rebels 2014–2023. Recaptured by FAMa/Wagner November 14, 2023. Wagner skull flag hoisted November 22, 2023.

label: Timbuktu
sub: Ancient city liberated from jihadists January 27, 2013. Under JNIM blockade since 2024; supply lines cut. Russian Africa Corps airbase struck by JNIM June 2, 2025; 60+ FAMa killed.

label: Mopti
sub: Epicenter of Katiba Macina / Fulani jihad. Amadou Koufa's home region. First zone of Wagner deployment (December 2021). Hub for Dogon-Fulani inter-communal violence. Near the Moura massacre site.

label: Ménaka
sub: IS-Sahel / ISGS operational heartland. Controls Gao-Ménaka corridor and access to the Liptako-Gourma tri-border zone. Sustained ISGS-FAMa fighting since 2017.

label: Tessalit
sub: Former French/US drone and intelligence base near the Algerian border. Former MINUSMA camp now occupied by FAMa and Africa Corps.

label: Konna
sub: Site of the January 10, 2013 jihadist advance that triggered Operation Serval. Amadou Koufa briefly declared 'Sultan of Konna.' Retaken by FAMa/French January 17, 2013.

label: Sévaré
sub: Key airfield 70 km south of Konna; the only military-capable airport outside Bamako. JNIM's capture of Konna in January 2013 put Sévaré within striking distance, triggering France's emergency intervention.

label: Diabaly
sub: Second jihadist axis of advance in January 2013. MUJAO forces captured Diabaly before being halted by French airstrikes. Its fall threatened the strategic Ségou-Bamako corridor.

label: Moura
sub: Site of the March 27–31, 2022 Moura massacre. FAMa and Wagner forces killed at least 500 civilians according to UN OHCHR. The single worst atrocity of the Sahel conflict. Malian junta blocked investigation.

label: Aguelhok
sub: Site of January 24, 2012 massacre of ~82 Malian soldiers by MNLA and Ansar Dine. Former MINUSMA camp. Now under FAMa/Africa Corps control.

label: Kati Garrison
sub: Mali's largest military garrison, 15 km from Bamako. Site of the March 2012 mutiny that launched Sanogo's coup. JNIM VBIED attack September 17, 2024 — first strike on the Bamako area in the war.

label: Ogossagou
sub: Fulani village in Mopti Region. Site of March 23, 2019 Ogossagou massacre — ~160 Fulani civilians killed by Dogon hunters' militia (Dan Nan Ambassagou). Worst inter-communal atrocity in modern Malian history.

label: Tongo Tongo
sub: Village in Niger's Tillabéri Region. Site of October 4, 2017 ISGS ambush that killed 4 US Special Forces soldiers and 4 Nigerien troops — the largest US combat loss in Africa since Somalia 1993.

label: Inates
sub: Nigerien military camp in Tillabéri Region. Site of December 10, 2019 ISGS assault killing 71 soldiers — the deadliest single attack in Niger's military history. Prompted ECOWAS emergency summit.

label: Tillabéri Region
sub: Northwestern Niger; IS-Sahel / ISGS operational heartland. Borders the Liptako-Gourma tri-border zone. Sustained attacks on military bases 2017–present. US Air Base 101 in nearby Niamey provided drone coverage until 2024.

label: Liptako-Gourma (Tri-Border)
sub: Mali-Niger-Burkina Faso border zone. Historical ISGS stronghold and most violent area of the Sahel conflict. Hundreds of attacks annually; estimated thousands of civilians killed 2017–2024.

label: Djibo
sub: Provincial capital in Burkina Faso's Sahel Region. Effectively besieged by JNIM since 2022; resupply only by air. Symbol of state collapse in northern Burkina Faso.

label: Dori
sub: Capital of Burkina Faso's Sahel Region. Hub of JNIM activity in northeastern Burkina. Periodic blockades and road ambushes have isolated surrounding communities.

label: Kaya
sub: Key logistics hub in Burkina Faso's Centre-Nord Region. JNIM convoy ambushes on the Ouagadougou-Kaya-Dori road have blocked humanitarian and military resupply to the north since 2022.

label: Fada N'Gourma
sub: Capital of Burkina Faso's Est Region. Gateway to the W National Park zone and the JNIM/IS-Sahel operational corridor toward Benin, Togo, and Ghana.

label: Pama (W Park Area)
sub: Southern Burkina Faso near the W National Park. JNIM/IS-Sahel operating zone for attacks into northern Benin. A Benin military convoy was ambushed here, killing 54 soldiers in 2025.

label: Boulkessi
sub: Malian military base near the Burkina Faso border. JNIM assault June 1, 2025: 60+ FAMa killed, ~220 taken hostage, weapons and vehicles seized. Among the heaviest single-day Malian military losses.

label: Douentza
sub: Town in Mopti Region. Captured by MUJAO in September 2012, retaken by French/Malian forces in 2013. Remains a contested corridor between Mopti and Gao.

label: Gourma-Rharous
sub: Timbuktu Region cercle. Major JNIM activity zone along the Niger River corridor. Site of multiple JNIM operations blocking Timbuktu resupply since 2023.

label: Ansongo
sub: Gao Region town on the Mali-Niger border. Contested JNIM/IS-Sahel zone. Bridge crossing for fighters between Mali and Niger. Multiple FAMa convoy ambushes 2019–2024.

label: Hombori
sub: Gao Region village. Site of July 2023 HRW-documented atrocities by Malian army and apparent Wagner fighters. New pattern of civilian killings emerged in surrounding area 2023.

label: Gossi
sub: Former French Barkhane base in the Gourma Region. MINUSMA camp also present. Site of documented Wagner Group incident in April 2022 when Russia staged fake 'mass grave' propaganda after French withdrawal.

label: Anéfis
sub: Staging point for the October–November 2023 FAMa/Wagner offensive toward Kidal. CSP-PSD Tuareg rebels held this town temporarily before the Wagner advance.

label: Ber
sub: Village near Timbuktu on the JNIM supply-interdiction corridor. Site of repeated FAMa convoy ambushes as JNIM tightened the Timbuktu siege 2023–2024.

label: Ségou
sub: Fourth-largest Malian city. Ségou Region is a major JNIM expansion zone, linking Katiba Macina operations in Mopti to growing activity in western Mali toward Senegal and Mauritania.

label: Nara
sub: Mali-Mauritania border area. JNIM expanding westward into Kayes and Nara regions since 2023. Multiple attacks on gendarmerie posts and convoys. Threatens Mauritanian border security.

label: Niafunké
sub: Timbuktu Region town. Birthplace of Amadou Koufa (from nearby Saraféré). Fulani pastoral recruitment heartland for Katiba Macina. Suffered repeated JNIM-aligned blockades of market access.

label: Ouahigouya
sub: Capital of Burkina Faso's Nord Region. JNIM expansion zone in northern Burkina Faso. Supply routes to surrounding villages repeatedly ambushed; surrounding schools closed.

label: Titao
sub: Loroum Province, Burkina Faso. JNIM and IS-Sahel activities in this corridor connecting the Sahel and Nord regions. Multiple VDP and FAB ambushes 2021–2024.

label: Ouallam
sub: Tillabéri Region, Niger. IS-Sahel attack corridor toward Niamey. Multiple military base attacks and civilian massacres in surrounding area 2019–2024.

label: Diffa
sub: Niger's southeast region. Secondary threat zone — Boko Haram / ISWAP activities from Lake Chad basin, overlapping with IS-Sahel expansion eastward.

label: Agadez
sub: Niger's major northern city. Site of US Air Base 201 — a $110M drone and intelligence facility from which Reaper drones tracked JNIM and ISGS leadership. US completed withdrawal August 2024.

label: Tahoua
sub: Central Niger region experiencing spillover IS-Sahel violence from Tillabéri. IS-Sahel expansion corridor toward Tahoua documented by ACLED from 2021.

label: Téra
sub: Tillabéri Region town on the Mali-Niger border. ISGS / IS-Sahel activity zone. Site of December 2021 attack on security force and civilians during an AU democracy march (69+ killed).

label: N'Djamena
sub: Capital of Chad; Operation Barkhane's permanent headquarters 2014–2022. French forces still present (reduced to ~1,000). Hub for remaining French regional operations in Africa.

label: Africa Corps Compound (Bamako)
sub: Russia's Africa Corps (formerly Wagner) permanent compound in Bamako. Satellite imagery confirms expanded facilities. Main coordination base for ~1,000-1,500 Africa Corps personnel in Mali.

label: Bobo-Dioulasso
sub: Burkina Faso's second city; economic hub. JNIM expansion threatening approaches to Bobo-Dioulasso from the north and west. Multiple attacks on surrounding villages 2022–2024.

label: Kongoussi
sub: Centre-Nord Region, Burkina Faso. JNIM attacks on gendarmerie and villages in Kongoussi area; gateway town on the deteriorating Kaya-Dori security corridor.

label: Pô
sub: Southern Burkina Faso near Ghana border. JNIM expansion southward threatening the Ghana and Togo borders. Attacks on gendarmerie posts 2023–2024.

label: Macina
sub: Historical name for central Mali; birthplace of the 19th-century Fulani Macina Empire that Amadou Koufa invokes as ideological justification for Katiba Macina. JNIM expansion corridor from Ségou to Mopti.

label: Bankass
sub: Mopti Region cercle near the Dogon Country and Burkina Faso border. Intense JNIM/Katiba Macina activity; multiple attacks on security posts 2018–2024.

label: Djenné
sub: Ancient UNESCO World Heritage city in Mopti Region; cercle encompassing the Moura massacre site. JNIM controls surrounding rural areas; Djenné itself has been under threat since 2018.

label: Bourem
sub: Gao Region town on the Niger River. JNIM and IS-Sahel both active in Bourem cercle. Transit corridor for armed groups between Gao and Kidal regions.

label: Tenenkou
sub: Mopti Region cercle. Core Katiba Macina territory; Fulani pastoral community stronghold for JNIM recruitment. Multiple attacks on security forces 2016–2024.

label: Nampala
sub: Ségou Region border town near Mauritania. JNIM base camp area. Malian military base at Nampala repeatedly attacked 2016–2020; 25+ soldiers killed in single 2016 attack.

label: Nioro du Sahel
sub: Kayes Region town near the Mauritanian border. JNIM westward expansion front. Attacks on gendarmerie and civilians increased 2023–2025 as JNIM expanded toward Senegal.

label: Mondoro
sub: Mopti Region near Burkina Faso border. Major JNIM transit and logistics base for cross-border operations between central Mali and northern Burkina Faso.

label: MINUSMA HQ (Former)
sub: MINUSMA headquarters in Bamako. Mission operated July 2013–December 2023. Largest UN peacekeeping mission budget in history (~$1.2B/year). 304 total deaths including 175 from hostile acts.

label: Splendid Hotel Attack Site
sub: Central Ouagadougou. Site of January 15, 2016 AQIM/al-Mourabitoun attack killing 30 people — Burkina Faso's worst terrorist atrocity at the time and signal of jihadist southward expansion.

label: Radisson Blu Attack Site
sub: Bamako. Site of November 20, 2015 AQIM/al-Mourabitoun hotel siege killing 22 people including diplomats and UN staff. Demonstrated jihadist ability to strike the capital.

label: Diré
sub: Timbuktu Region town on the Niger River. JNIM transit corridor between Timbuktu and western Mali. Supply route interdiction operations cut off Diré from 2023.

label: Nondin / Soro
sub: Villages in Burkina Faso, Sissili Province. Site of February 25, 2024 massacre of 223 civilians (including 56 children) by Burkinabè forces and VDP militias — called potential crimes against humanity by HRW.

label: Camp Firgun (Former Barkhane Base)
sub: France's main operational base for Operation Barkhane in Mali, at Gao airport. Transferred to MINUSMA and FAMa following France's 2022 withdrawal. Now used by FAMa and Africa Corps.

label: Taoudenni
sub: Remote northern Mali region with significant gold deposits. FAMa and Africa Corps extending control northward after Kidal capture. Previously inaccessible state authority zone.

label: Ménaka (IS-Sahel Stronghold)
sub: IS-Sahel province administrative center. Adnan Abu Walid al-Sahrawi's primary operational zone before his 2021 killing. Massive civilian displacement from IS-Sahel ethnic targeting campaigns 2019–2022.

label: Faramana
sub: Burkina Faso-Mali border crossing. JNIM use this corridor to coordinate operations between central Mali and western Burkina Faso. Customs post repeatedly attacked 2022–2024.

label: Bourzanga
sub: Bam Province, Burkina Faso. Site of a January 2023 VDP convoy massacre by JNIM — among the worst single-incident civilian losses in Burkina Faso.

label: Léré
sub: Tombouctou Region town on the Mauritanian border. Site of early MNLA attacks January 2012. Targeted by French airstrikes in the first hours of Operation Serval January 11, 2013.

label: Sokolo
sub: Ségou Region, Mali. JNIM attacks on Sokolo gendarmerie multiple times 2019–2023. Major Katiba Macina recruitment and attack corridor between Mopti and Ségou regions.
